fixes_confirmed: Hash deep-linking FIXED — useState initializer at line 679-682 now reads window.location.hash on mount; sharing #dashboard URL actually lands on Dashboard. Finally.
still_broken: overflow:visible bleed — CONFIRMED in desktop screenshot: SUNRISE element and plant sprites visibly extend past the purple office-floor border on the right and bottom edges. Office.css line 84. Review #8+, now ESCALATED to critical.

Triple visibilitychange listeners — still 3 separate useEffect hooks registering 3 independent listeners: line 859 (handleVisibilityResume), line 881 (handleVisibilityChange for interval), line 1100 (handleVisibilityChange for title). All have cleanup, but triple-registration is needless.

completionToast 16-property inline style block — App.jsx lines 1149-1166. Flagged in #58 AND #59, still untouched. position, bottom, left, transform, background, border, borderRadius, padding, fontSize, fontWeight, color, boxShadow, zIndex, whiteSpace, pointerEvents, animation — all static or derivable via CSS custom property.

Duplicate data-vibe CSS selectors — Office.css lines 91-95 use single-quoted attribute selectors (dead, overridden); lines 97-115 use double-quoted selectors (active). The 5 single-quote rules at lines 91-95 are entirely unreachable CSS. Flagged in #59, still not deleted.

Tab panel ARIA broken — ternary at App.jsx line 1258 still conditionally renders one tabpanel at a time. aria-controls on the inactive tab button points to a DOM node that does not exist. WCAG 4.1.2 violation.
polling-paused-pill is a <span> with onClick (App.jsx line 1194) — not focusable, not keyboard-activatable. Flagged in #59, still not a <button>.

window.matchMedia() called inline during render — App.jsx line 1201 evaluates window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches synchronously inside JSX on every render. Should be hoisted to a module constant or media query hook.
No H2 headings — 'Active Workers' (line 1267) and 'Activity Log' (line 1333) are still <div className='section-title'> instead of <h2>. Document outline jumps from H1 to nothing.
new_issues: sparkleUnseen uses useState but the setter is never called (App.jsx line 721). const [sparkleUnseen] = useState(...) — this is just a lazy-initialized constant masquerading as state. Use const sparkleUnseen = !localStorage.getItem('footer-sparkle-seen') or a useRef; currently adds an unnecessary React state slot.

Activity log filter logic computed twice per render — lines 1335-1349 compute the filtered+capped count inline for the label span, then lines 1394-1403 re-apply the exact same filter to render the list. Extract to a useMemo([activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter]) shared by both sites.

stat-dispatch 'active' span has onClick but is not a button (App.jsx line 478-482) — style={{ cursor: 'pointer' }} and onClick to scroll to workers grid, but it's a <span>, so keyboard users can't trigger it. Make it a <button> or add role='button' tabIndex={0} onKeyDown handler.

getRelativeTime duplicated as inline IIFE in Office.jsx lines 740-749 — identical logic to App.jsx lines 55-64, copied verbatim into the ghost-ooo-bubble render path. Export getRelativeTime from App.jsx (or a shared utils file) and import it in Office.jsx.

tab-error-badge has no accessible label (App.jsx line 1254) — {errorCount} announces just a number to screen readers with zero context. Add aria-label={`${errorCount} error${errorCount !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`}.

Array.from({ length: 12 }) for ambient particles allocates a new array on every render (App.jsx line 1124). The particles array is static — extract it to a module-level constant: const PARTICLE_INDICES = Array.from({ length: 12 }, (_, i) => i). Same applies to confetti (length 30) if it fires frequently.

Mobile: speech bubble still overlaps Office tab button — confirmed in screenshot: Bloberto's speech bubble ('Wait you shipped at THIS hour?! Legends. 🏆') renders above the office-floor container and pushes into the tab-toggle row, partially obscuring the 'Office' tab label. The tab-toggle needs a higher z-index or the speech bubble needs a top-clamp.

suggestions.title: office-floor overflow:visible — boundary bleed confirmed (review #8+ and counting)
suggestions.description: Office.css line 84: `.office-floor { overflow: visible; }` — confirmed visually in QA #60 screenshot: the SUNRISE gradient element extends past the right border of the container, and plant/cactus sprites appear outside the bottom edge. This has been flagged in at least 8 consecutive reviews. Escalating to critical. Fix: change to `overflow: clip` which clips layout overflow but still allows absolutely-positioned children (speech bubbles, tooltips) to escape. If full overflow is intentional for specific elements, add a CSS comment explaining why and scope it — but the current blunt `overflow: visible` is clearly wrong for a bordered rounded container.

suggestions.title: Tab panel ARIA pattern broken — aria-controls targets ghost DOM node
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1258: the two tabpanels are rendered with a ternary — only the active panel exists in the DOM. When Office is active, `aria-controls='tabpanel-dashboard'` on the Dashboard tab button points to a non-existent element. Screen readers silently fail. WCAG 4.1.2 violation. Fix: render BOTH panels always and hide the inactive one: `<div role='tabpanel' id='tabpanel-office' hidden={tab !== 'office'}>`. The `hidden` attribute prevents the inactive content from being visible or interactive while keeping the ARIA reference valid. This was flagged in #59 as high — escalating to critical because it affects every screen reader user every time they look at the tab bar.

suggestions.title: polling-paused-pill is a non-interactive <span> — keyboard users cannot resume polling
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1194-1202: `<span className='polling-paused-pill' onClick={onResume}>` — a span is not focusable and cannot be activated via keyboard. A user relying on Tab/Space/Enter navigation can never resume polling once it pauses. Fix: change to `<button className='polling-paused-pill' type='button' onClick={onResume}>`. Also remove `aria-live='polite'` from this element — live regions should be passive containers, not interactive controls; the live announcement belongs on a separate sr-only span. Flagged in #59, not yet fixed.

suggestions.title: Duplicate data-vibe CSS selectors — 5 dead rules at lines 91-95 of Office.css
suggestions.description: Office.css defines the same 5 data-vibe attribute selectors twice: lines 91-95 use single-quoted syntax with only border-color and box-shadow; lines 97-115 use double-quoted syntax with the same properties PLUS --grid-line. CSS cascade means the double-quote block at lines 97-115 wins every time. The 5 single-quote rules (crushing, on-fire, in-flow, slow-day, after-hours) at lines 91-95 are entirely dead. Delete them. Zero risk, pure cleanup. Flagged in #59, still sitting there.

suggestions.title: Triple visibilitychange listeners — consolidate into one useEffect
suggestions.description: App.jsx has three separate useEffect hooks each registering a visibilitychange event listener: line 859 (handleVisibilityResume — resume polling), line 881 (handleVisibilityChange — reset polling interval), line 1100 (handleVisibilityChange — update document.title). All three have cleanup, so there are no leaks, but triple-registration is unnecessary overhead and makes the visibility control flow hard to reason about. Consolidate into a single useEffect that handles all three behaviors. Flagged in #59 as medium — elevating to high because it compounds with window.matchMedia in render and duplicate filter computation to create an unnecessary performance profile.

suggestions.title: No H2 headings — major sections use <div className='section-title'>
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1267 and 1333: 'Active Workers (N)' and 'Activity Log' are both rendered as `<div className='section-title'>`. The document has one H1 ('Bloberto's Office') and zero H2s, making screen reader heading navigation useless. Fix: change both `<div className='section-title'>` to `<h2 className='section-title'>`. The CSS rule `.section-title` continues to work unchanged. Visual appearance: identical. Accessibility: dramatically improved. Flagged in #59, still unaddressed.

suggestions.title: completionToast has a 16-property inline style block — flagged since review #58
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1149-1166: the completionToast `<div>` carries a style={{}} object with 16 properties: position, bottom, left, transform, background, border, borderRadius, padding, fontSize, fontWeight, color, boxShadow, zIndex, whiteSpace, pointerEvents, animation. Only border and boxShadow are dynamic (depend on completionToast.type / completionToast.color). Fix: create `.completion-toast` in App.css for all static properties and pass dynamic values via CSS custom properties: `style={{ '--toast-color': completionToast.type === 'info' ? '#6b7280' : (completionToast.color || '#6366f1') }}`. This has been flagged in both #58 and #59 with zero movement. Assign and close.

suggestions.title: window.matchMedia() called inline during render (App.jsx line 1201)
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1201: `window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches` is evaluated synchronously inside JSX during every render cycle. This triggers a synchronous style resolution on each re-render. Fix: hoist to a module-level constant — `const IS_TOUCH_DEVICE = typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches` — or use a custom hook if the value needs to update dynamically (it won't in practice). The value is also separately called in Office.jsx line 703 as `window.matchMedia('(hover: none)').matches`, which is a different check but the same pattern. Flagged in #59, unchanged.

suggestions.title: Activity log filter computed twice per render — extract to useMemo
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1335-1349 compute the filtered+capped activity log inline inside a JSX expression to derive a display count string. Then lines 1394-1403 apply the exact same activityFilter + workerFilter predicates again to render the actual list. This is two O(n) passes over activityLog on every render. Fix: extract to `const filteredActivity = useMemo(() => activityLog.filter(...).filter(...), [activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter])` and use it in both places. This also eliminates the code duplication.

suggestions.title: tab-error-badge has no accessible label — screen readers read a bare number
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1254: `{errorCount > 0 && {errorCount}}` — the badge renders inside the Dashboard tab button with only the raw count. A screen reader would announce something like 'Dashboard tab, 2' with no indication that '2' means error count. Add `aria-label={`${errorCount} error${errorCount !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`}` to the span so it reads 'Dashboard tab, 2 errors'.

suggestions.title: Mobile: speech bubble overlaps Office tab button — tab label partially obscured
suggestions.description: Confirmed in QA #60 mobile screenshot: Bloberto's speech bubble ('Wait you shipped at THIS hour?! Legends. 🏆') renders in the space above the office-floor container and visually overlaps the tab-toggle row, obscuring the 'Office' tab label. The tab-toggle needs a higher z-index than speech bubbles (or speech bubbles need a top-boundary clamp). Add `z-index: 10` to `.tab-toggle` in App.css (it currently has no z-index declaration) and/or add `max-top: 0` clamping to `.speech-bubble` positioning so it cannot overflow above the office-floor boundary.

suggestions.title: sparkleUnseen uses useState with no setter — should be a const
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 721: `const [sparkleUnseen] = useState(() => !localStorage.getItem('footer-sparkle-seen'))` — the state setter is never destructured and the value never changes after mount. This is using useState for a one-time-read constant, adding an unnecessary React state slot. Replace with: `const sparkleUnseen = !localStorage.getItem('footer-sparkle-seen')` (computed once when the component mounts). The lazy initializer behavior is preserved since this is inside a function component; the localStorage read runs once per mount. No behavior change, one less state slot.

suggestions.title: getRelativeTime duplicated as inline IIFE in Office.jsx
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 740-749 implement the exact same relative-time calculation as App.jsx lines 55-64 (getRelativeTime function) — verbatim, as an inline IIFE inside the ghost-ooo-bubble render path. Office.jsx has no import of getRelativeTime. Fix: move getRelativeTime to `src/utils/time.js` (or append to constants.js), export it, import it in both App.jsx and Office.jsx. One source of truth, easier to update if the thresholds change.

suggestions.title: Array.from({ length: 12 }) for ambient particles allocates on every render
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1124: `Array.from({ length: 12 }, (_, i) => ...)` inside the main return statement allocates a new array on every render cycle. The particle count is static. Extract to a module-level constant: `const PARTICLE_INDICES = Array.from({ length: 12 }, (_, i) => i)` and map over that. The confetti arrays (length 30, lines 1130 and 1137) are inside conditional renders that don't fire often, so they're lower priority — but the ambient particles render on every re-render of the App component.

suggestions.title: stat-dispatch active span with onClick is not keyboard accessible
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 478-482: `<span className='stat-dispatch' style={{ cursor: 'pointer' }} onClick={() => document.querySelector('.workers-grid')?.scrollIntoView(...)}>` — a span with onClick but no tabIndex, no role, no onKeyDown. Keyboard users cannot trigger the scroll-to-workers behavior. Fix: change to `<button className='stat-dispatch stat-dispatch--link' type='button' onClick={...}>` or add `role='button' tabIndex={0}` with an onKeyDown handler. Low priority because it's a convenience scroll, not core functionality.

suggestions.title: Scattered static inline styles throughout worker card and LoadingSkeleton JSX
suggestions.description: Multiple locations with static (non-dynamic) inline styles that belong in CSS: (1) WorkerCard `worker-info` div: `style={{ flex: 1, paddingLeft: '0.75rem' }}` (line 307); (2) activity icon span: `style={{ marginRight: '0.35rem' }}` (line 377); (3) back-time span: `style={{ opacity: 0.5, fontSize: '0.7rem', marginLeft: '0.35rem' }}` (line 379); (4) LoadingSkeleton has ~8 inline style objects (lines 551-560) for skeleton dimensions — these are perfect candidates for CSS custom properties or utility classes. None of these values are dynamic. Moving them to CSS reduces JSX noise and enables easier theming.
